hypothesis: If CHROMA pairs a frozen Llama encoder with a trainable Transformer decoder over a discrete material–thickness vocabulary and its capacity is increased, then it will translate natural-language color prompts into multilayer thin-film designs that achieve systematically lower CIE76 ΔE on both paraphrase and novel-target splits and maintain low ΔE across 0–60° viewing angles, because richer language representations and decoder capacity better map linguistic color constraints to physically valid optical stacks.
